schemes.short_description: Scholarship for meritorious students from economically weaker sections studying in classes 9-12.
schemes.detailed_description: The National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship Scheme aims to award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop out at class VIII and encourage them to continue the study at secondary stage. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹12,000 per annum to selected students studying in classes IX to XII in government, government aided and local body schools.
key_benefits: Annual scholarship of ₹12,000 (₹1,000 per month for 12 months)
Covers tuition fees and other educational expenses
Encourages continuation of education at secondary level
Direct transfer to student's bank account
schemes.income_criteria: Parental income not exceeding ₹3.5 Lakhs per annum
other_conditions: Must have secured at least 55% marks or equivalent grade in Class VIII examination
For SC/ST students, minimum marks requirement is 50%
Must be studying in government, government aided or local body schools
Students studying in Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas and Kendriya Vidyalayas are not eligible

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of securing minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST)
Certificate showing parental income not exceeding ₹3.5 Lakhs per annum
Student's bank account for scholarship transfer

application_steps_online: Visit the National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Click on 'New Registration' and fill in basic details
Verify email and mobile number through OTP
Login with credentials and select NMMSS scheme
Fill application form with personal, academic and bank details
Upload required documents (marksheet, income certificate, etc.)
Submit application and note down application ID for future reference
application_steps_offline: Contact your school principal or district education officer
Fill the form with accurate details
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents
Submit to school authorities before deadline

submission_locations.notes: School will verify and forward applications to district authorities

faq.question: Can private school students apply?
faq.answer: No, only students from government, government aided and local body schools are eligible.

faq.question: Is the scholarship renewable?
faq.answer: Yes, if the student maintains satisfactory academic performance and continues in eligible schools.

common_rejection_reasons: Income certificate showing income above ₹3.5 Lakhs
Marks below minimum requirement (55% for general, 50% for SC/ST)
Studying in ineligible schools (private, KV, JNV)
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure income certificate is recent (within 6 months)
Double-check all uploaded documents are clear and readable
Keep application ID safe for tracking
